route.name: Bay Area Triple Crossover
route.region: Bay Area / Peninsula / East Bay / Marin
route.description: Official/public GPX long Bay Area traverse with dirt, trail, pavement connectors, and bridge/transit logistics.
route.surfaceMix: Official/public GPX long Bay Area traverse with dirt, trail, pavement connectors, and bridge/transit logistics.
route.bestSeason: Mostly year-round outside storm and fire-weather windows; summer requires heat, water, and campsite/lodging checks.

difficultyProfile.surface: Official/public GPX long Bay Area traverse with dirt, trail, pavement connectors, and bridge/transit logistics.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Urban and park water exists unevenly; measure gaps and verify current park services.
resources.food: Many town/service options near the route, but exact stops and hours need planning.
resources.camping: Multi-night legal camp/lodging sequence must be confirmed before gate confirmation.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before gate confirmation.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Treat as a serious multi-day local traverse, not a beginner route.
Use the official GPX as the geometry source.
Plan transit/parking and legal overnight stops before promotion.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control gate confirmation.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: promote to plan_ready source coverage only; gate status remains blocked until date-specific gates pass

days.name: Bay Area Triple Crossover segment 1

waypoints.name: Restroom by the Tilden Steam Train parking lot
Water fountain by the Tilden Steam Train parking lot
Water spigot 100ft before entrance of Redwood Gate
Water fountain at Clyde Woolridge Staging Area
Water fountain at William Cann Neighborhood Park
Panoramic city views on the descent through Garin Regional Park
Don Edwards San Francisco Bay National Wildlife Refuge
Easy to miss fork onto Jewell Trail
Water fountain at Deer Park trailhead
Hike-a-bike for 0.2 miles up a steep trail entering Five Canyons Open Space

logistics: Final gate confirmation requires live date-specific resource and legal checks.

Samuel P. Taylor source checked July 2, 2026: https://www.parks.ca.gov/?page_id=469

Plan Ready pilot source audit checked July 5, 2026 for Bay Area Triple Crossover.

Bailout source: Marin Transit routes 61/68 and broader Bay Area transit are source-of-truth starting points for Marin/Point Reyes exits; exact segment-specific bailout plan remains itinerary-specific.
Parking source: GGNRA/NPS maps and California State Parks pages identify public access and parking sources for Marin/Point Reyes anchors.

readinessEvidence.finding: Imported source note identifies the route as 161 miles, 3-4 days, 65% unpaved, and 14,860 feet of ascent; full-route next-weekend use requires a 4-day plan or shorter segment.
Samuel P. Taylor and Mount Tamalpais official availability both show NO for Friday July 10 and Saturday July 11; no route-wide camp sequence is confirmed.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Samuel P. Taylor and Mount Tamalpais pages

readinessEvidence.finding: Mount Tam rules allow bicycles only on fire roads and Coast View Trail, so any Mount Tam section must be audited against the official bike-legal corridors.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Mount Tamalpais page

readinessEvidence.finding: NPS conditions page points visitors to forecast checks; departure-week weather/fire checks remain unresolved for a multi-jurisdiction route.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Golden Gate National Recreation Area alerts and conditions page

readinessEvidence.finding: Subagent found public GPX/artifacts pass, but the full 161-mile route needs a verified 4-day itinerary or shorter segment for the target window.
No confirmed July 10-13 camp/lodging sequence; North Bay state-park camp anchors are blocked.
Obvious North Bay and Half Moon Bay state-park anchors are unavailable or first-come-only for critical July 10-12 dates.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Samuel P. Taylor and Half Moon Bay

readinessEvidence.finding: Public route source identifies the Bay Area Triple Crossover route identity and source route context.
Mount Tamalpais State Park page is an official source for bike-legality constraints, camping, parking, water/facilities, and current trail closure context for the Marin section.
Samuel P. Taylor State Park page is an official source for Cross Marin/Bill's Trail/fire-road bike rules, camping, parking, drinking water, and restrictions for the Point Reyes connector corridor.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks Samuel P. Taylor

readinessEvidence.finding: GGNRA conditions page is the official source for Golden Gate/Marin Headlands closure and alert refreshes.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Golden Gate National Recreation Area alerts and conditions

readinessEvidence.finding: NWS San Francisco Bay Area office is the weather/fire-weather source for route-wide departure-window checks.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: National Weather Service San Francisco Bay Area

readinessEvidence.finding: AirNow and CAL FIRE are the smoke/AQI and wildfire incident sources for route-wide departure-window checks.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: AirNow and CAL FIRE current incidents

readinessEvidence.finding: California State Parks Samuel P. Taylor and Mount Tamalpais pages identify official camping/permit source paths, including ReserveCalifornia links and first-come/hiker-bike mechanics where applicable; exact nights remain date-specific.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks / ReserveCalifornia camping sources

readinessEvidence.finding: State Parks pages for Samuel P. Taylor and Mount Tamalpais list drinking water/facilities; NPS Point Reyes basic information identifies surrounding community services for resupply context.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: California State Parks and NPS Point Reyes services context

readinessEvidence.finding: Marin Transit West Marin Stagecoach routes 61 and 68 provide daily rural service and bike racks for Mt. Tam, Stinson/Bolinas, Fairfax, Lagunitas, Olema, Point Reyes, and Inverness bailout corridors.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Marin Transit West Marin Stagecoach routes

readinessEvidence.finding: GGNRA maps and California State Parks pages identify public access/parking source pages for Marin Headlands, Mount Tam, and Samuel P. Taylor route anchors; exact start/finish lot remains itinerary-specific.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: NPS GGNRA maps and California State Parks parking/access pages

readinessEvidence.finding: Local static build/export pipeline verifies map artifacts for the imported route; exact navigational export QA remains local-artifact based.
